items.headline: City addresses affordable housing protections when subsidies end
items.summary: The council discussed new policies to protect affordable housing units if project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects existing affordable housing stock and what happens to residents if funding agreements end.

items.headline: Storm sewer system master plan moving forward with consultant
items.summary: The city is amending its agreement with Wood Rodgers, Inc. to continue the city-wide master planning for storm sewer infrastructure. This planning will guide major stormwater management improvements across San José.

items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital projects and spending plans
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to examine Mountain View's capital improvement program for the next fiscal year, which outlines major infrastructure and development projects. This is the planning phase where residents can learn what physical improvements the city is prioritizing for the coming year.

items.headline: City Council to Revise Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to its Residential Tenant Protections Program on April 14. The amendments could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in the city.

items.headline: City releases youth services and library facilities improvement plans
items.summary: The council received status reports on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan and the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. These outline the city's vision for improving services and facilities for young people and library users.

items.headline: Downtown historic building eligible for major floor area bonus
items.summary: The City Council is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow the property owner to add 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for historic and seismic rehabilitation. This could enable substantial redevelopment of a downtown property while preserving its historic character.

items.headline: City of Cupertino launches summer jobs program for youth
items.summary: The City Council received a presentation on a new Summer Jobs program. This initiative provides employment opportunities for young residents during the summer months.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The City Council will hold a public hearing on this significant rezoning and project proposal.
